response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a highly experienced engineer with Staff-level tenure at Google and Meta, but his career is firmly rooted in web, TypeScript, and Node.js development. C++ does not appear in his skills list, and his only substantive C++ exposure is a six-month role at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a university thesis project. There is no evidence of industrial automation knowledge, IoT protocol experience (MQTT, OPC UA, Modbus, IEC 61850), secure development lifecycle practices (IEC 62443), or embedded/concurrent systems design at the level this role demands. The gap between his actual background and the core requirements of this position is too wide to bridge at interview.
strengths: Staff-level engineering seniority with demonstrated high-ownership delivery at top-tier companies (Google, Meta)
Hands-on experience building high-throughput, low-latency systems at Dunnhumby using async I/O, caching, and concurrent architectures
Strong event-driven and distributed-systems thinking evidenced by the open-source Geo-Data platform and microservices work
concerns: C++ is absent from his stated skills list; verifiable C++ work amounts to one six-month role in 2014–2015 and a thesis project
No exposure to industrial automation, IEC 62443 cybersecurity standards, or IoT protocols (MQTT, OPC UA, Modbus, DNP 3.0)
Career trajectory is consistently web/TypeScript/cloud-native, which is fundamentally misaligned with on-premises industrial C++ platform development
